entities.name: CATTLE
entities.description: Bovine animals used in livestock production systems.

entities.name: HEAT STRESS
entities.description: Physiological stress caused by high temperature and humidity that reduces feed intake.

entities.name: DRY MATTER INTAKE
entities.description: The amount of feed consumed by an animal, excluding water, used as a measure of feed intake.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE-HUMIDITY INDEX
entities.description: A reliable variable used for the measurement of heat stress in cattle.

entities.name: META-ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to assess the relationship between dry matter intake and the temperature-humidity index globally.

entities.name: SYSTEMATIC LITERATURE REVIEW
entities.description: A structured method used to gather data for the meta-analysis.

entities.name: PHYSICAL COOLING INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: Interventions such as shade or sprinklers used to alleviate heat stress.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The driver of increased frequency and severity of heat waves.

entities.name: ANIMAL GENOTYPES
entities.description: The genetic variation among cattle that influences the effect of heat stress.

entities.name: LACTATION STAGE
entities.description: The stage of milk production which affects the relationship between THI and DMI.

entities.name: PARITY
entities.description: The number of times a cow has given birth, used as a covariation factor in the analysis.
